---
title: Thornton Lab
format:
    html:
        format-links: false
---

Welcome to the **Thornton Lab** at Corane Farm Research. We study the developmental assembly of sensorimotor circuits and activity-dependent synaptic connectivity, combining electron microscopy connectomics with in vivo physiology and computation.

## Research interests

- Developmental wiring of sensorimotor circuits
- Activity-dependent synaptogenesis and circuit refinement
- Electron microscopy connectomics at synaptic resolution
- Computational models of circuit assembly rules

## Recent news

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| 2026-03-01 | ConnectTrack paper accepted at *Nature Neuroscience* |
| 2026-01-10 | Søren Nielsen joins the lab as a research specialist |
| 2025-11-15 | Dr. Ingrid Solberg moves to University of Oslo as Assistant Professor |
| 2025-09-05 | SynaptogenEM poster presented at SfN Annual Meeting |
| 2025-06-20 | DevCircuit preprint posted on bioRxiv |

## Contact

- **Email:** [thornton@coranefarm.org](mailto:thornton@coranefarm.org)
- **Office:** Corane Farm Research, Building B, Room 212
- **Address:** 19700 Double Lane, Selden, MD 20147
